FILE SS-Ω NOV
Lore / Novel / Ch. 5 · Trace Debt

Debt Protocol Book 1 Ch. 5

Debt Protocol · Chapter 5

Trace Debt

Debt Protocol · Book 1 · Chapter 5

Re: Debt Protocol · Chapter 5

From: Debt Protocol · archived transmission

Subject: Trace Debt

SS-Ω Debt Protocol · Book 1 · Chapter 5

Ch. 05 Page 1

The first sign the day was wrong came as a polite notification.

Ch. 05 Page 2

Readout

[LOCKER ACCESS ALERT]
Med Corridor B / Locker cluster
Routine inventory pass scheduled: 00:42:11

Max stared at the pane while standing in line for synthetic coffee that tasted like boiled wires.

Munch read it over his shoulder and said exactly one word.

Ch. 05 Page 3

"No."

Ch. 05 Page 4

Maxine looked up from his wrist display.

"How routine is routine?"

Munch's jaw tightened.

"Routine enough to include random confiscation."

Ch. 05 Page 5

Max dismissed the pane.

Ch. 05 Page 6

It returned immediately with the same timer.

00:42:03.

He dismissed it again.

Ch. 05 Page 7

00:41:58.

Ch. 05 Page 8

"It's pinning," he said.

Maxine took a slow breath.

"Then we move the cache."

Ch. 05 Page 9

Munch shook her head.

Ch. 05 Page 10

"Move now and we walk straight into pre-inventory patrol pattern."

"Leave it and we trust routine."

"We don't trust routine."

Max looked from one to the other, coffee packet going warm in his hand.

Ch. 05 Page 11

The station around them did what stations do:

Ch. 05 Page 12

Workers pushed carts. Drones swept lanes. People pretended not to overhear fear.

The overhead cycle board shifted lines in flat white text:

MID-CYCLE DEBT RECONCILIATION QUALITY ASSURANCE SWEEPS

Ch. 05 Page 13

"That's inventory language," Munch said.

Ch. 05 Page 14

Maxine answered.

"It's seizure language."

Max didn't drink the coffee.

He poured it into a drain and watched brown foam spin away.

Ch. 05 Page 15

"How long to route to B corridor without touching monitored lanes?"

Ch. 05 Page 16

Munch pulled up map layers.

"Fastest clean path is twelve minutes if we split."

Maxine looked at Max.

Ch. 05 Page 17

"You're still tracked active. Your path pings no matter what."

Ch. 05 Page 18

"Then we use that."

Munch turned to him.

"Use how?"

Ch. 05 Page 19

"Maxine baits the tracker down main with me visible. You slip service and pull cache."

Ch. 05 Page 20

Maxine frowned.

"Reverse that. I'm tracked heavier."

"Exactly. You draw more eyes."

He was right and everyone hated it.

Ch. 05 Page 21

Munch slid her gaze between them, calculating risk like a person balancing glass on moving steel.

Ch. 05 Page 22

"Timer's running."

00:40:29.

"Fine," she said. "Maxine and Max go loud lane. I go spine C and hit B-17 from the back."

Ch. 05 Page 23

Max nodded.

Ch. 05 Page 24

The plan sat wrong in his chest, not because it was stupid, but because it repeated an old pattern: someone he cared about walking into danger while he stayed where the cameras could see him behave. Visibility was its own kind of cowardice here. He swallowed the objection. Objections without better tactics were just noise.

"What if locker is already tagged?"

Munch's expression didn't move.

"Then I leave with nothing and we pretend this was a walk."

Ch. 05 Page 25

Maxine clipped his gauntlets in place.

Ch. 05 Page 26

"And if you don't check in?"

Munch holstered her torch.

"You burn the route map and start a riot."

Ch. 05 Page 27

Maxine grunted.

Ch. 05 Page 28

"Reasonable."

Main lane was theater.

Max and Maxine walked shoulder to shoulder through the brightest part of Dock Ring C while Max's tracked token icon blinked on a three-second rhythm.

Ch. 05 Page 29

Blink. Pause. Blink.

Ch. 05 Page 30

Every blink felt like a finger in his back.

V.A.L.U. chimed at cheerful volume.

"Asset #864, thank you for maintaining visible route compliance!"

Max spoke through his teeth.

Ch. 05 Page 31

"You're welcome."

Ch. 05 Page 32

"Sarcasm detected. Logging engagement."

Maxine leaned close enough for private comms.

"Keep talking to it."

Ch. 05 Page 33

"Why?"

Ch. 05 Page 34

"Makes you look obedient."

"I'm not obedient."

"I know. We just need them to think you are for ten minutes."

They passed disbursement lane A.

Ch. 05 Page 35

A woman in a medic stripe suit was arguing with a kiosk while two drones recorded from opposite angles.

Ch. 05 Page 36

"I paid! I paid the minimum!"

The kiosk responded:

Readout

[PAYMENT ACKNOWLEDGED]
Additional stabilization surcharge due.
Ch. 05 Page 37

The woman slammed her fist into the glass.

Ch. 05 Page 38

The drones moved in.

Max kept his eyes forward for three strides. On the fourth he looked back, just long enough to fix her face in memory, the way you'd memorize a witness you might need later. Maxine didn't scold him…

for it. That was almost worse. It meant even small mercies had been priced into the plan.

Ch. 05 Page 39

He kept walking because stopping would be a statement.

Ch. 05 Page 40

He hated that he knew that now.

Maxine steered him around a cargo tug and into the open plaza beneath the claim board.

Sixteen slots floated. Ten occupied. Two flashing red.

An overhead announcement cut in:

Ch. 05 Page 41

"Attention assets: quality assurance sweep in Med Corridor B in thirty minutes. Please report personal storage claims proactively to reduce friction."

Ch. 05 Page 42

Max did not look at Maxine.

Maxine did not look at Max.

They both heard it.

Ch. 05 Page 43

"Can QA announce exact corridor like that?" Max asked.

Ch. 05 Page 44

"Only when they want panic traffic to reveal outliers."

"Great."

"Always."

They looped once around the claim board, then doubled back past Salvage Yard to burn clock and produce clean telemetry.

Ch. 05 Page 45

Max's tracked token changed color from blue to pale green.

Ch. 05 Page 46

Readout

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Route regularity: improving

"I am going to punch the person who wrote this UI," Max said.

Maxine kept his voice flat.

Ch. 05 Page 47

"Take a number."

Ch. 05 Page 48

Munch moved through service spine C like she had built it herself.

He only heard her in short private bursts in his left audio channel.

"Crossed junction." "One drone overhead, no pause." "At ladder drop."

Ch. 05 Page 49

Then silence for thirty seconds.

Ch. 05 Page 50

Max's pulse climbed.

"Munch?"

Static.

Maxine didn't break stride.

Ch. 05 Page 51

"She mutes during fine work."

Ch. 05 Page 52

Twenty seconds later:

"At locker row. Two workers nearby. Waiting cycle."

Max exhaled.

Ch. 05 Page 53

Maxine said, "Good."

Ch. 05 Page 54

Munch:

"Locker cluster scanner is running full pass already. Early start."

Maxine stopped dead.

"How early."

Ch. 05 Page 55

"Now."

Ch. 05 Page 56

Max looked at the timer pane still pinned in his corner.

00:31:02.

"They moved it up."

Ch. 05 Page 57

Munch's voice dropped.

Ch. 05 Page 58

"Locker B-17 is flagged yellow."

Max's mouth went dry.

"Can you pull it?"

Ch. 05 Page 59

"Trying."

Ch. 05 Page 60

He could hear faint tool sounds through the channel.

Click. Metal tap. Breath.

Then:

"No."

Ch. 05 Page 61

Maxine's shoulders tightened.

Ch. 05 Page 62

"No what."

"Panel's been replaced. New anti-tamper mesh inside. If I force it, local alarm."

Max's hands curled into fists.

Ch. 05 Page 63

"Then we lost it."

Ch. 05 Page 64

"Not yet."

Munch sounded like she was moving.

"There's a maintenance vent behind the locker wall. If I can reach from rear conduit, I might pull the shim blind."

Maxine nodded once, even though she couldn't see.

Ch. 05 Page 65

"Do it."

Ch. 05 Page 66

"Thirty seconds."

Max counted heartbeats.

At twelve, V.A.L.U. interrupted.

Ch. 05 Page 67

"Asset #864, your route regularity has improved by 14%! Keep up the responsible behavior!"

Ch. 05 Page 68

Max wanted to bite through his own tongue.

Maxine hissed, "Play nice."

"Thanks, V.A.L.U.," Max said.

Ch. 05 Page 69

"You're welcome!"

Ch. 05 Page 70

At twenty-seven heartbeats, Munch came back on channel, breathing hard.

"Got it."

Max stopped walking.

"Say again."

Ch. 05 Page 71

"Got the shim. Almost dropped it into coolant return. It smells like dead batteries and shame, but it's in my glove."

Ch. 05 Page 72

Maxine actually smiled for half a second.

"Route out?"

"Complicated."

Ch. 05 Page 73

"How complicated."

Ch. 05 Page 74

"Two QA officers entered corridor. They're scanning random lockers manually."

Max's HUD flashed.

Readout

[PROXIMITY ALERT]
Linked teammate anomaly risk: elevated

"It can see your risk now," Max said.

Ch. 05 Page 75

"It can always see risk," Maxine said. "Now it monetizes it in real time."

Ch. 05 Page 76

Munch cut in.

"Need a distraction in three minutes or I get trapped between scanner lanes."

Maxine looked toward the claim board and then up at a hanging cable crane with overloaded cargo.

Ch. 05 Page 77

"I can give you noise."

Ch. 05 Page 78

"Not fire," Munch said immediately.

"No fire."

"No injuries."

Ch. 05 Page 79

"I said no fire."

Ch. 05 Page 80

Maxine cracked his neck.

"Max, with me."

Maxine's plan was simple and therefore terrible.

At the edge of claim board lane, he pointed at a red-flashing derelict slot and said, loudly:

Ch. 05 Page 81

"That fighter is mis-tagged. It's stealing my claim line."

Ch. 05 Page 82

A nearby asset turned.

"No it's not."

Maxine stepped forward.

Ch. 05 Page 83

"Read the registry hash."

Ch. 05 Page 84

"I can't from here."

"Then stop talking."

Within fifteen seconds three more people had stopped to watch.

Within twenty-five seconds two drones had rolled in for dispute recording.

Ch. 05 Page 85

Within forty seconds V.A.L.U. appeared on public speakers:

Ch. 05 Page 86

"Attention assets! Please resolve claim disagreements using approved civility pathways."

Maxine leaned in harder, voice rising exactly to the level that looked authentic and drew supervisors.

"Approved civility can kiss vacuum. That tag is wrong."

Ch. 05 Page 87

The crowd swelled.

Ch. 05 Page 88

Drones multiplied.

Max stood one step behind Maxine and played confused muscle.

His tracked token stayed green.

Ch. 05 Page 89

Readout

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Conflict proximity detected.
No direct aggression logged.
Ch. 05 Page 90

Good.

While the lane watched Maxine argue with a hologram, Munch's whisper came through:

"Passing scanner lane two."

Five seconds later:

Ch. 05 Page 91

"Past QA officers."

Ch. 05 Page 92

Ten seconds:

"Out of B corridor. Moving to yard route."

Maxine kept yelling for another full minute, then let a supervisor drone "win" by offering to file a formal complaint.

Ch. 05 Page 93

He accepted.

Ch. 05 Page 94

"Fine. File it."

The crowd dispersed instantly, disappointed and relieved.

Maxine stepped back beside Max and lowered his voice.

"Where is she."

Ch. 05 Page 95

"Moving yard route."

Ch. 05 Page 96

"Good."

A new pane appeared in Max's corner.

Readout

[CONDUCT NOTICE]
You remained non-violent during a conflict event.
Behavior credit: +1
Ch. 05 Page 97

Max laughed, genuinely this time.

Ch. 05 Page 98

"Behavior credit. Amazing."

Maxine rolled his eyes.

"Don't spend it all in one place."

Ch. 05 Page 99

They regrouped behind recycler stack two, out of primary sightlines but still in noise.

Ch. 05 Page 100

Munch arrived two minutes late, breathing hard, visor streaked with coolant mist.

She held up her glove.

Inside, nested in folded mesh cloth, was the storage shim.

Max almost sagged.

Ch. 05 Page 101

"You did it."

Ch. 05 Page 102

"Don't praise me until we verify files."

Maxine looked at the corridor.

"Not here."

Ch. 05 Page 103

"Safe room?"

Ch. 05 Page 104

"Too obvious."

Munch shook her head.

"We use Marta."

Max blinked.

Ch. 05 Page 105

"Marta? Yard ops Marta?"

Ch. 05 Page 106

"Marta has a dead printer terminal with no net uplink and old physical ports."

"Why would she help."

Munch gave him a look.

Ch. 05 Page 107

"Because she hates mixed payout math almost as much as she hates management."

Ch. 05 Page 108

"That's not a reason."

"In this sector it is."

Marta did not greet them.

Ch. 05 Page 109

She was elbow-deep in a sorter tray full of bent tech components and didn't look up when they approached.

Ch. 05 Page 110

"Not buying. Not selling. Come back on cycle quarter."

Munch put both palms on the counter.

"Need your printer terminal."

Marta finally glanced up.

Ch. 05 Page 111

"No."

Ch. 05 Page 112

Maxine leaned one arm on the side rail.

"We'll pay."

"No."

Ch. 05 Page 113

Max slid his tracked token icon into low visibility mode with a tap and tried his luck.

Ch. 05 Page 114

"Please."

Marta stared at him for a long second, then snorted.

"Please costs extra."

Munch pulled a sealed tube from her belt pouch and set it on the counter.

Ch. 05 Page 115

Marta's eyes flicked to it.

Ch. 05 Page 116

"What is that."

"Pre-collapse thermal paste. Real silver suspension."

Marta went still.

Ch. 05 Page 117

"You're lying."

Ch. 05 Page 118

Munch unscrewed the cap enough for the smell to hit.

Marta's expression changed from skepticism to hunger.

"...Fine," she said. "Three minutes. If drones ask, you're here for valuation dispute."

Ch. 05 Page 119

She jerked her chin toward a side bay and keyed a hidden panel.

Ch. 05 Page 120

A narrow room opened behind stacked crates.

Inside sat an old print-and-log station with a cracked keyboard, dead fan, and a side slot that looked exactly like the shim's form factor.

"Three minutes," Marta repeated. "Then I never saw you."

Munch inserted the shim.

Ch. 05 Page 121

The terminal did not boot.

Ch. 05 Page 122

Maxine swore.

Munch hit the chassis twice with the flat of her hand.

The screen flickered. Coughed. Came alive in grayscale.

Ch. 05 Page 123

Readout

LOCAL PRINT NODE
NO NETWORK
INSERT MEDIA
Ch. 05 Page 124

Max pointed.

"Media's in."

The node hesitated, then mounted the files.

Readout

3 FILES FOUND
1) mirror_index_cap1.img
2) mirror_index_cap2.img
3) process_tag_cap3.img
Ch. 05 Page 125

Munch selected print all.

Ch. 05 Page 126

The printer head screamed to life like an animal being dragged backward through time.

Page one spat out, grainy but legible:

MAX_102 ... TERMINATED MAX_233 ... DEFAULT RECOVERY MAX_377 ... COGNITIVE PACK APPLIED MAX_612 ... MISSING / UNRESOLVED MAX_864 ... ACTIVE

Ch. 05 Page 127

Page two:

Ch. 05 Page 128

timestamp clusters. branch IDs. failure tags.

Page three:

DELTA_MIRROR_ROUTER

Ch. 05 Page 129

Under it, a line Max hadn't seen in the tiny capture:

Ch. 05 Page 130

policy: retention by debt cohort

Max stared.

"Debt cohort."

Maxine said, "Means they bucket us by balance and run outcomes."

Ch. 05 Page 131

Munch corrected him.

Ch. 05 Page 132

"Means they bucket us by value density."

"What's value density."

Munch didn't look away from the page.

Ch. 05 Page 133

"How much suffering per credit returned."

Ch. 05 Page 134

Nobody spoke for two full seconds.

Then the side door banged open and Marta hissed:

"Out. Now."

A drone ping sounded in the yard outside.

Ch. 05 Page 135

Marta shoved a brown envelope at Munch.

Ch. 05 Page 136

"Put paper in this. If that tracker sees open print stock on you, you get searched."

Munch stuffed the three pages in and zipped it inside her suit.

Max yanked the shim.

Ch. 05 Page 137

Maxine unplugged power to the printer station.

Ch. 05 Page 138

The screen died.

They stepped out into the yard just as a patrol drone rolled past the bay entrance.

The drone paused.

Ch. 05 Page 139

Lens swept.

Ch. 05 Page 140

Marta was already shouting at Max before the lens settled.

"I told you valuation mismatch isn't my problem! Take your complaint to lane F!"

Max caught on.

"Lane F said lane C!"

Ch. 05 Page 141

"Then lane C can kiss my actuator!"

Ch. 05 Page 142

The drone logged:

Readout

[OBSERVATION]
Customer service dispute.
No escalation.

It rolled on.

Ch. 05 Page 143

Marta waited until it was gone, then stabbed a finger at the exit.

Ch. 05 Page 144

"Get out."

Maxine set the thermal paste on her counter.

"Pleasure doing business."

Marta didn't smile.

Ch. 05 Page 145

"Never again."

Ch. 05 Page 146

Munch nodded once.

"Until next time."

Marta muttered something rude and went back to her sorter tray.

Ch. 05 Page 147

They didn't stop moving until they reached the lower med corridor dead zone where wall scanners were known to drop packets under compressor load.

Ch. 05 Page 148

Maxine posted at the corner.

Munch pulled the envelope out and spread the pages on a maintenance crate.

Max looked at them under bare utility light.

Ch. 05 Page 149

The paper made it real in a way panes never did.

Ch. 05 Page 150

Ink. Fibers. Edges.

No easy delete.

No automatic corruption countdown.

He touched the first line.

Ch. 05 Page 151

MAX_102.

Ch. 05 Page 152

"How many are there total?"

Munch traced the edge of page two.

"At least six in this snapshot. Maybe more off-page."

Ch. 05 Page 153

Maxine said quietly:

Ch. 05 Page 154

"Enough."

Max looked at process tag line again.

policy line: retention by debt cohort

"Retention for what?"

Ch. 05 Page 155

Maxine answered first.

Ch. 05 Page 156

"For yield."

Munch answered second.

"For control."

Ch. 05 Page 157

Max answered last.

Ch. 05 Page 158

"For repetition."

Groundhog day.

Loop after loop. Not accident. Policy.

Ch. 05 Page 159

His HUD flashed with a new mid-cycle statement pane:

Ch. 05 Page 160

Readout

[CYCLE STATUS]
Debt cohort assignment: updated

Munch saw it at the same moment.

"They moved your cohort."

"Based on what?"

Ch. 05 Page 161

Maxine looked at the pages.

Ch. 05 Page 162

"Based on this."

"They know we printed?"

"Maybe."

Ch. 05 Page 163

Munch folded the pages back into the envelope and sealed it.

Ch. 05 Page 164

"Then we stop carrying all three together."

She split them:

Page one to Max. Page two to Maxine. Page three to herself.

"If one of us gets taken, two pages survive."

Ch. 05 Page 165

Maxine nodded.

Ch. 05 Page 166

"Good."

Max slid his page into chest seam.

It scratched against skin through fabric.

Ch. 05 Page 167

Good.

Ch. 05 Page 168

He wanted to feel it there.

Wanted proof to hurt.

The next problem arrived before they made it back upstairs.

Ch. 05 Page 169

A system broadcast on all local channels, voice neutral:

Ch. 05 Page 170

"Attention assets in Sector 7: unscheduled audit refinement begins now. All tracked-active tokens report for calibration."

Max stopped walking.

The tracked token icon in his HUD turned amber.

Readout

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Mandatory calibration in: 00:14:00
Ch. 05 Page 171

Maxine cursed.

Ch. 05 Page 172

"Too fast."

Munch's eyes were already scanning route options.

"If he misses mandatory, it escalates to containment."

Ch. 05 Page 173

Max looked at both of them.

Ch. 05 Page 174

"Then I go."

"Not with page one on you," Munch said.

Maxine held out his hand.

"Trade."

Ch. 05 Page 175

Max hesitated a beat, then swapped his page for Maxine's page two.

Ch. 05 Page 176

Less identifying. Still evidence.

Munch tucked page three deeper and zipped her suit higher.

"Calibration room means scans, posture checks, cognitive prompts. You stay dumb and compliant."

Ch. 05 Page 177

"Again?"

Ch. 05 Page 178

"Again."

Maxine clapped Max once on the shoulder.

"And if they ask why your route moved weirdly this morning?"

Ch. 05 Page 179

Max answered automatically.

Ch. 05 Page 180

"Service corridor. Looking for a place to throw up."

Maxine almost smiled.

"Now you're learning."

Calibration Bay 2 was not the same as review bays.

Ch. 05 Page 181

No table. No chairs.

Ch. 05 Page 182

Just a standing ring with floor markers, three scanner columns, and one wall screen displaying a rotating silhouette of a human body with problem zones highlighted in yellow.

A technician in gray compliance coveralls waved Max onto the center marker without making eye contact.

"Arms out."

Ch. 05 Page 183

Max held his arms out.

Ch. 05 Page 184

White light scanned top to bottom.

The tech watched numbers on a wrist slate and chewed the inside of his cheek.

"Tilt head left."

Max tilted.

Ch. 05 Page 185

"Right."

Ch. 05 Page 186

Tilt.

"Hold gaze on blue point."

A blue dot appeared on the wall screen.

Ch. 05 Page 187

Max stared.

Ch. 05 Page 188

Questions flashed in rapid sequence, too fast to process fully:

Readout

Do you hear unlogged voices? Y/N
Do you perceive duplicate prompts? Y/N
Do you recall non-sanctioned memories during runs? Y/N
Would you accept stabilization for improved performance? Y/N

Max answered:

Ch. 05 Page 189

N. N. N. N.

Ch. 05 Page 190

The wall screen changed.

Readout

[CALIBRATION RESULT]
Token remains active.
Anomaly pressure: persistent.
Recommendation: monitored assignment.

The technician finally looked at him.

"You keep declining packs, they'll escalate recommendation."

Ch. 05 Page 191

Max kept his voice flat.

Ch. 05 Page 192

"Noted."

"You should just take it."

"You take one?"

Ch. 05 Page 193

The technician looked away.

Ch. 05 Page 194

"Not my bracket."

He tapped completion.

"Go."

Max stepped out of the ring feeling like he'd been measured for a coffin.

Ch. 05 Page 195

Maxine and Munch were waiting where they said they'd be, near a dead vending unit that still advertised:

Ch. 05 Page 196

ZERO CALORIES / MAX COMPLIANCE

Munch's first question:

"Did they touch your head?"

Ch. 05 Page 197

"Only with questions."

Ch. 05 Page 198

"Any new penalties?"

Max checked.

Readout

[TOKEN STATUS]
Monitored assignment: ENABLED
Ch. 05 Page 199

"Monitored assignment."

Ch. 05 Page 200

Maxine spat to the side.

"Means they can force target picks."

Max blinked.

"Force as in suggest?"

Ch. 05 Page 201

Maxine laughed once.

Ch. 05 Page 202

"Force as in legal fiction."

Munch looked up at cycle board timing and swore.

"Assignment queue opens in eight minutes."

Ch. 05 Page 203

"Can we skip run?"

Ch. 05 Page 204

"Not with active token."

Max rubbed his eyes.

"Great."

Maxine glanced down corridor, then back.

Ch. 05 Page 205

"One run. Clean. Then we plan next hatch."

Ch. 05 Page 206

Munch held his gaze for a long second.

"One run."

Max nodded, because motion was the only available prayer.

Ch. 05 Page 207

Assignment queue was a new cruelty.

Ch. 05 Page 208

Tracked-active assets stood in a separate line while a wall display assigned targets in real time.

No choice wheel. No reroll. No negotiation.

When Max's tag hit the scanner, the wall flashed:

Ch. 05 Page 209

Readout

[ASSIGNMENT]
Target: F-11 (Uncommon Derelict)
Risk band: Yellow/Red
Compliance reason: Route regularity test
Ch. 05 Page 210

Max stared.

"Route regularity test."

Munch read over his shoulder.

"They're stress-testing your pattern."

Ch. 05 Page 211

Maxine looked up at the target map.

Ch. 05 Page 212

"F-11 drifts near debris shear lane."

Max laughed without humor.

"So they picked weather."

Ch. 05 Page 213

"They picked whether you'll obey in weather."

Ch. 05 Page 214

The launch cradle opened beneath them with a mechanical thud.

No countdown.

Just go.

Max climbed into the pilot seat and felt page two in his pocket press against his thigh.

Ch. 05 Page 215

Proof on paper. Debt in lights. Loop in steel.

Ch. 05 Page 216

The skiff sealed.

HUD bloomed.

Readout

[FORCED ASSIGNMENT RUN]
Round 1/10
Token monitor: active
Penalty carryover: active
Ch. 05 Page 217

Draw:

Ch. 05 Page 218

Readout

1) REPAIR
2) SCAN
3) EXTRACT

Same verbs.

Different teeth.

Ch. 05 Page 219

Max looked through the canopy.

Ch. 05 Page 220

F-11 rotated ahead in the dark, hull plating glittering where old impacts had stripped paint to bare metal.

Groundhog day.

Only now he knew the loop had a policy name.

He dragged SCAN into play.

Ch. 05 Page 221

The beam fired.

Ch. 05 Page 222

And before results came back, a whisper slid across his HUD in thin gray text that did not belong to any approved pane:

if this run breaches twice, they will move you to retention path c

Max's breath caught.

Ch. 05 Page 223

Munch saw his posture change.

Ch. 05 Page 224

"What."

He kept his eyes forward.

"Nothing."

Maxine from behind:

Ch. 05 Page 225

"Lie better."

Ch. 05 Page 226

The scan result hit the screen.

Readout

[SCAN RESULT]
Primary pocket: unstable
Secondary pocket: hidden
Advisory: shield recommended

No shield in draw.

Ch. 05 Page 227

Of course.

Ch. 05 Page 228

Max's tracked token pulsed amber.

Readout

[TOKEN NOTE]
Proceed with assignment priorities.

He touched REPAIR.

Ch. 05 Page 229

Microdrones launched.

Ch. 05 Page 230

Hull ticked up.

The skiff entered shear-lane fringe.

External debris began to patter against the shieldless canopy like hard rain.

Rain.

Ch. 05 Page 231

The word hit him in two places at once:

Ch. 05 Page 232

memory and weather.

Hard-won and immediate.

He tightened his grip, eyes locked on the next prompt as F-11 turned broadside and the first breach window of the run opened like a mouth.

Ch. 05 Page 233

The token pulsed again.

Ch. 05 Page 234

Readout

[ASSIGNMENT PRIORITY]
Extract from primary pocket.
Deviation will be logged.

Max stared at it.

Primary pocket was unstable.

Secondary pocket was hidden.

Ch. 05 Page 235

"Token wants primary," he said.

Ch. 05 Page 236

Munch swore.

"Of course it does."

Maxine leaned forward.

Ch. 05 Page 237

"You can deviate once without immediate lock, maybe twice if your behavior score is still positive."

Ch. 05 Page 238

Max glanced at the behavior credit in his corner panel.

+1.

One fake good-boy point between him and whatever retention path c meant.

Ch. 05 Page 239

He selected secondary pocket anyway.

Ch. 05 Page 240

The skiff yawed left.

Clamps snapped onto a seam the scanner had tagged as low-visibility.

For one breath everything held.

Then the token pane flashed red.

Ch. 05 Page 241

Readout

[DEVIATION LOGGED]
Assignment compliance reduced.
Ch. 05 Page 242

"One," Maxine said.

"I heard it."

The hidden pocket opened with a hiss and vented frozen particulate across the canopy in a white spray.

Ch. 05 Page 243

Inside floated three sealed containers and one loose panel with faded lettering:

Ch. 05 Page 244

RETEN...

The rest was gone.

Max reached for EXTRACT.

No EXTRACT in active draw.

Ch. 05 Page 245

He had burned it.

Ch. 05 Page 246

New draw spooled:

Readout

1) SCAVENGE
2) REPAIR
3) EXTRACT

He almost laughed.

Ch. 05 Page 247

The system waited until he saw the label before giving him a way out.

Ch. 05 Page 248

Classic.

A danger card struck before he could play.

Readout

[DANGER EVENT]
DEBRIS SHEAR FRONT
Impact in 00:00:09
Ch. 05 Page 249

The canopy lit with incoming fragments.

Ch. 05 Page 250

Maxine snapped, "Extract now."

Max hit EXTRACT.

Die.

Readout

[RESULT]
41/100
Threshold: 35
BREACH AVOIDED
Ch. 05 Page 251

Close enough to feel in your teeth.

Ch. 05 Page 252

Loot pane:

Readout

[SALVAGE SECURED]
Scrap +103
Metal +2
Tech +1

Then immediately:

Ch. 05 Page 253

Readout

[LIEN TAX]
Scrap -22
Auto-service transfer complete.
Ch. 05 Page 254

Max watched the subtraction and felt his pulse stabilize into anger.

Token note followed:

Readout

[TOKEN NOTE]
Deviation tolerated.
Further deviation increases retention risk.

"One tolerated," Maxine said.

Ch. 05 Page 255

"You narrating now?"

Ch. 05 Page 256

"You hearing now."

Round 2 opened.

Readout

[ROUND 2/10]
1) REPAIR
2) EXTRACT
3) SCAN
Ch. 05 Page 257

Max chose REPAIR first.

Ch. 05 Page 258

If the system wanted obedience, he'd give it clean hulls and ugly compliance.

Repair landed.

Hull to 71%.

Ch. 05 Page 259

Then SCAN.

Ch. 05 Page 260

This time both pockets were marked unstable.

No hidden route. No trick.

Token priority appeared again:

Readout

[ASSIGNMENT PRIORITY]
Primary pocket required.
Ch. 05 Page 261

"If you deviate again?" Max asked.

Ch. 05 Page 262

Munch answered.

"We find out what retention c is."

Max selected primary.

Ch. 05 Page 263

The extractor engaged.

Ch. 05 Page 264

The hull groaned.

He extracted.

Die.

Readout

[RESULT]
37/100
BREACH AVOIDED
Ch. 05 Page 265

Too close.

Ch. 05 Page 266

Too often.

He started to understand why everyone in this sector moved like they were always bracing for impact.

Round 3 gave him SHIELD.

Ch. 05 Page 267

Mercy card.

Ch. 05 Page 268

He took it.

Shields: 1.

Then another token note:

Ch. 05 Page 269

Readout

[TOKEN NOTE]
Compliant behavior trend improving.
Ch. 05 Page 270

"It likes me now," Max said.

Maxine's laugh was harsh.

"It likes your data."

Round 3 breach roll:

Ch. 05 Page 271

Readout

[RESULT]
54/100
BREACH AVOIDED
Ch. 05 Page 272

No hit.

No shield burn.

The shield sat there unused, and Max hated how much comfort that gave him.

Ch. 05 Page 273

Round 4 opened with thin energy and a thick warning.

Ch. 05 Page 274

Readout

[ROUND 4/10]
Energy: 58/85
Shear lane severity: escalating

Draw:

Readout

1) REPAIR
2) EXTRACT
3) EXTRACT

Again with the double-extract architecture.

Ch. 05 Page 275

Groundhog day.

Ch. 05 Page 276

Card glow. Choice.

He forced himself to REPAIR first.

Then single EXTRACT.

Ch. 05 Page 277

Roll:

Ch. 05 Page 278

Readout

[RESULT]
32/100
BREACH TRIGGERED

Shield flared.

Absorbed most.

Ch. 05 Page 279

Hull still dropped.

Ch. 05 Page 280

Readout

[HULL]
63%

Crew loss roll ran.

Readout

[CREW LOSS]
Roll: 76/100
No immediate loss.

Max exhaled through his nose and ignored the second EXTRACT card until it timed out.

Ch. 05 Page 281

Token pane noted:

Ch. 05 Page 282

Readout

[TOKEN NOTE]
Risk impulse suppressed.

"Did it just grade my restraint?" Max asked.

Munch said, "Yes."

Ch. 05 Page 283

"I hate that."

Ch. 05 Page 284

"So does everyone."

Round 5 started with a whisper.

Not voice this time.

A HUD artifact at the edge of the draw pane:

Ch. 05 Page 285

retention path c includes memory normalization

Ch. 05 Page 286

Then gone.

Max didn't react.

Could not react.

Ch. 05 Page 287

The token was watching expression drift now; he could feel it.

Ch. 05 Page 288

He played boring.

REPAIR. SCAN. EXTRACT.

The run got meaner anyway.

Ch. 05 Page 289

At extraction:

Ch. 05 Page 290

Readout

[RESULT]
35/100
Threshold: 35
BREACH TRIGGERED

Exact threshold.

No shield active.

The skiff bucked hard enough that Max's teeth clicked.

Ch. 05 Page 291

Munch slammed a palm to the console to keep from being thrown.

Ch. 05 Page 292

Maxine punched emergency patch.

Crew roll:

Readout

[CREW LOSS]
Roll: 45/100
Survivor status retained.
Ch. 05 Page 293

Token flashed yellow.

Ch. 05 Page 294

Readout

[RETENTION RISK]
Increasing.

"Two breaches," Maxine said.

"I can count," Max snapped.

"Then count how many they said triggers path c."

Ch. 05 Page 295

Max swallowed.

Ch. 05 Page 296

The whisper had said if this run breaches twice.

It had breached twice.

Round 6 draw came up with no shield, no repair.

Ch. 05 Page 297

Readout

1) SCAN
2) EXTRACT
3) SCAVENGE
Ch. 05 Page 298

Munch looked at him.

"Extract fast. Minimize events."

Maxine added:

Ch. 05 Page 299

"No scavenge. No greed."

Ch. 05 Page 300

Max hit EXTRACT immediately.

Die.

Readout

[RESULT]
60/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Good.

Ch. 05 Page 301

Too late to matter.

Ch. 05 Page 302

Token pane:

Readout

[RETENTION REVIEW]
Pending.

Round 7 and 8 passed with clean rolls.

Ch. 05 Page 303

Round 9 almost broke them.

Ch. 05 Page 304

Shear front warning hit mid-draw.

No shield.

Hull at 57%.

Max pulled EXTRACT on instinct and prayed to nobody.

Ch. 05 Page 305

Roll:

Ch. 05 Page 306

Readout

[RESULT]
36/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Again one point from disaster.

Maxine laughed, tired and sharp.

Ch. 05 Page 307

"You're being measured in millimeters."

Ch. 05 Page 308

Max wanted to answer and couldn't find a word that wasn't violence.

Round 10 came with a forced prompt before cards.

Readout

[PRE-ROUND NOTICE]
Retention review will resolve at run completion.
Ch. 05 Page 309

Then draw:

Ch. 05 Page 310

Readout

1) REPAIR
2) EXTRACT
3) SCAN (DEEP)

SCAN (DEEP) glowed like a confession booth.

Munch shook her head.

"No."

Ch. 05 Page 311

Maxine:

Ch. 05 Page 312

"Absolutely no."

Max selected REPAIR.

Hull to 64%.

Ch. 05 Page 313

Then EXTRACT.

Ch. 05 Page 314

Final roll:

Readout

[RESULT]
48/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Run complete.

No fanfare.

Ch. 05 Page 315

Then the pane they were waiting for:

Ch. 05 Page 316

Readout

[RETENTION REVIEW RESOLUTION]
Path C deferred.
Status: WATCHLIST
Condition: zero additional anomaly incidents next cycle.

Max stared until his eyes hurt.

"Deferred."

Ch. 05 Page 317

Munch let out a slow breath she probably hadn't had since round five.

Ch. 05 Page 318

Maxine checked the watchlist icon that replaced token status.

"You get one bad day."

"I already had one."

Ch. 05 Page 319

"You get one more."

Ch. 05 Page 320

The skiff auto-routed back to Dock Ring C.

V.A.L.U. resumed cheerful tone as if none of it mattered.

"Great work, Asset #864! You have demonstrated resilience under dynamic constraints."

Max said nothing.

Ch. 05 Page 321

He was busy memorizing the exact shape of the word deferred.

Ch. 05 Page 322

Dock Ring C had a new queue by the time they landed.

WATCHLIST ASSETS / MID-CYCLE MANDATORY CHECK

Of course.

Ch. 05 Page 323

Maxine looked at the sign and muttered, "They're not subtle."

Ch. 05 Page 324

Munch said, "Subtle doesn't bill."

Max joined the line because refusal wasn't resistance, it was auto-escalation.

Three people ahead.

One came out crying. One came out blank. One didn't come out by the time Max went in.

Ch. 05 Page 325

Mandatory check bay was smaller than calibration bay and twice as bright.

Ch. 05 Page 326

A single officer in white-black sat behind a desk panel with no nameplate.

She didn't look up when Max entered.

"Asset #864. Confirm awareness of watchlist conditions."

Ch. 05 Page 327

Pane appeared:

Ch. 05 Page 328

Readout

[WATCHLIST CONDITIONS]
- No unauthorized interface access
- No route deviations without cause
- No unresolved audio anomalies
- Compliance response required within 3 seconds on prompt

"Confirm," she said.

Max said, "Confirm."

Ch. 05 Page 329

She swiped to next pane.

Ch. 05 Page 330

Readout

[OPTIONAL SUPPORT]
Cognitive stabilization remains available.

"Decline," Max said before she could ask.

Now she looked up.

"Why."

Ch. 05 Page 331

He held her gaze.

Ch. 05 Page 332

"I prefer continuity."

Her eyes narrowed a fraction.

"Continuity is expensive."

Ch. 05 Page 333

"So is amnesia."

Ch. 05 Page 334

She held his gaze another beat, then tapped complete.

"Check done. Return to operations."

He stepped out.

Munch and Maxine were at the end of the lane pretending not to wait.

Ch. 05 Page 335

Maxine asked, "Offer still live?"

Ch. 05 Page 336

"Yeah."

"You declined?"

"Yeah."

Ch. 05 Page 337

Munch nodded once.

Ch. 05 Page 338

"Good."

No praise.

Just confirmation.

Ch. 05 Page 339

The three of them moved toward lower med corridors without discussing it because there was no other next move.

Ch. 05 Page 340

They had three pages.

They had a watchlist.

They had maybe one cycle before path c stopped being deferred.

Lower med corridor B-17 was clear when they arrived.

Ch. 05 Page 341

No drones. No patrol. No sirens.

Ch. 05 Page 342

Too clean.

Max unlocked the locker with the analog key.

Inside:

Ch. 05 Page 343

torn glove. cracked med patch.

Ch. 05 Page 344

No shim.

Max froze.

"No no no."

Munch shoved in beside him and checked the back panel seam.

Ch. 05 Page 345

Empty.

Ch. 05 Page 346

Maxine scanned the floor, the wall, the adjacent lockers.

"Could've slipped."

"Could've been taken," Munch said.

Ch. 05 Page 347

Max's HUD pulsed.

Ch. 05 Page 348

A fresh pane opened with no sender metadata.

Readout

[NOTE]
don't cache where policy can inventory
locker was touched at 14:02
hatch 14 tonight or lose sequence

Maxine saw Max's face.

Ch. 05 Page 349

"What does it say."

Ch. 05 Page 350

Max read it out loud.

No more secrets inside the team.

Munch pressed the bridge of her nose.

"Touched at fourteen-oh-two. That's during QA sweep."

Ch. 05 Page 351

Maxine looked up corridor.

Ch. 05 Page 352

"Then they have the shim."

Max stared at the pane.

"It says lose sequence."

Ch. 05 Page 353

"Meaning?" Munch asked.

Ch. 05 Page 354

Max shook his head.

"Either message bait or timeline warning. I don't know."

Maxine made the decision like he was cutting wire.

"Then we go hatch 14 tonight."

Ch. 05 Page 355

Munch snapped toward him.

Ch. 05 Page 356

"That is what bait wants."

"And if it's not bait, waiting kills proof."

"They already took proof."

Ch. 05 Page 357

"We still have pages."

Ch. 05 Page 358

Max held up a hand.

"Stop."

They stopped.

Ch. 05 Page 359

He thought of the run. Watchlist. Path c deferred.

Ch. 05 Page 360

He thought of MAX_377. Pack applied.

He thought of the little gray whisper:

if this run breaches twice

And how it had been right.

Ch. 05 Page 361

"We go hatch 14," Max said.

Ch. 05 Page 362

Munch's eyes went hard.

"Watchlist condition one says no unauthorized interface access."

"Then don't authorize me."

Ch. 05 Page 363

Maxine almost smiled.

Ch. 05 Page 364

"Now he's thinking like debt."

Munch didn't smile back.

"We go smart or we don't go."

"Define smart."

Ch. 05 Page 365

"No tracked devices. No standard lanes. No repeat route from last time. Hard extraction path before entry."

Ch. 05 Page 366

Maxine nodded.

"Agreed."

Max added:

Ch. 05 Page 367

"And if we see stabilization hardware, we document first, run second."

Ch. 05 Page 368

Munch blinked.

"You think hatch 14 is tied to packs?"

"I think everything is tied to packs now."

Ch. 05 Page 369

Maxine checked cycle board timing on his wrist.

Ch. 05 Page 370

"Shift ghost in forty-nine minutes."

Munch re-locked the empty locker and pocketed the key.

"Then we prep."

Max glanced once at the empty shelf where the shim had been.

Ch. 05 Page 371

Proof lost. Trace gained.

Ch. 05 Page 372

Groundhog day.

But now the day was leaving him notes.

Prep happened in motion.

Ch. 05 Page 373

At Salvage Yard, Marta sold them two damaged comm beads with cracked housings and no registered serial IDs.

Ch. 05 Page 374

"Burners," she said. "Range sucks. Reliability worse."

Maxine took all three and cracked one open with his thumb.

"Good."

At a maintenance cage, Munch borrowed a coil of mag-line "for conduit work" and never returned it.

Ch. 05 Page 375

At an idle tool bench, Max stripped his wrist module to minimal mode so tracked token updates buffered instead of broadcasting live.

Ch. 05 Page 376

A warning flashed:

Readout

[TOKEN DESYNC RISK]

He accepted.

Ch. 05 Page 377

At the cycle board, shift ghost countdown dropped under ten minutes.

Ch. 05 Page 378

The ring changed temperature in tiny ways you could only feel if you'd been through enough of them:

patrols widened then tightened, camera sweeps lagged then corrected, workers moved faster because everyone knew the overlap window was either opportunity or funeral.

Munch distributed comm beads.

Ch. 05 Page 379

"Short bursts only. No names on open channel."

Ch. 05 Page 380

Maxine holstered one blade and left the second visible.

"If this goes loud, we don't hold hatch. We hold route."

Max nodded.

"If we get split?"

Ch. 05 Page 381

Munch answered:

Ch. 05 Page 382

"Service spine C fallback. If that's burned, med corridor waste chute."

Maxine:

"If both burned, run anywhere with people and make a scene."

Ch. 05 Page 383

Max looked at him.

Ch. 05 Page 384

"Scene?"

"Noise buys seconds."

The overhead board clicked:

SHIFT GHOST - 00:00:59

Ch. 05 Page 385

The ring lights dimmed one notch.

Ch. 05 Page 386

Max's HUD flashed one last watchlist reminder.

Readout

[WATCHLIST]
No unauthorized interface access.

He dismissed it.

Ch. 05 Page 387

This time, it stayed gone.

Ch. 05 Page 388

Shift ghost began.

Maxine looked at Max.

"Ready?"

Ch. 05 Page 389

Max checked his pockets:

Ch. 05 Page 390

page two. burner bead. nothing else.

No safety. No certainty.

"No," he said.

Then:

Ch. 05 Page 391

"Move."

Ch. 05 Page 392

Service spine C felt narrower at shift ghost.

Not physically. Psychologically.

Every corner looked like a decision tree. Every drone tone sounded like a question with penalties.

Ch. 05 Page 393

Maxine took lead. Munch took rear. Max ran center and tried not to breathe like prey.

Ch. 05 Page 394

The first camera spine at junction 3 was already desynced, sweeping ten degrees late.

Maxine timed it.

"Now."

They crossed.

Ch. 05 Page 395

The second spine had a live tech under it, kneeling with panel tools.

Ch. 05 Page 396

Munch switched channels.

"Detour."

Maxine shook his head.

Ch. 05 Page 397

"No time."

Ch. 05 Page 398

He stepped into full view and barked:

"Power drop in bay four, who signed this timing?"

The tech looked up, startled and annoyed.

Ch. 05 Page 399

"What power drop?"

Ch. 05 Page 400

"The one you miss if you keep your face in that panel."

The tech stood, swore, and hustled toward bay four without checking logs.

Maxine motioned.

"Go."

Ch. 05 Page 401

They slipped past.

Ch. 05 Page 402

Hatch 14 spur came into view.

The SEALED stencil looked freshly repainted.

Bad sign.

Ch. 05 Page 403

Worse sign: two new conduit lines had been run to the reader plate since last time.

Ch. 05 Page 404

Munch crouched.

"Reader's hot."

Maxine scanned corridor.

"Can you still bypass?"

Ch. 05 Page 405

"Can I? Yes."

Ch. 05 Page 406

"Can you quietly?"

Munch bared her teeth.

"Define quietly."

Ch. 05 Page 407

Max's HUD flickered.

Ch. 05 Page 408

A ghost pane appeared, thin gray text:

left conduit is dummy / right conduit carries callback

He swallowed.

Ch. 05 Page 409

"Right conduit is live callback."

Ch. 05 Page 410

Munch froze.

"How do you know."

"The note channel."

Maxine did not waste time asking if they trusted it.

Ch. 05 Page 411

"Cut left."

Ch. 05 Page 412

Munch clipped one line with insulated jaws.

No alarm.

She clipped the second.

Ch. 05 Page 413

Still no alarm.

Ch. 05 Page 414

"Both were dummy."

Maxine cursed.

Max's ghost text updated:

good / they wanted you to choose

Ch. 05 Page 415

His skin crawled.

Ch. 05 Page 416

"It says they wanted us to choose."

Munch pressed her forehead to the hatch for one second.

"I hate this."

Ch. 05 Page 417

Maxine:

Ch. 05 Page 418

"Open it."

Munch keyed her bypass probe.

The reader flashed yellow, then blue, then white.

Ch. 05 Page 419

Click.

Ch. 05 Page 420

Hatch 14 opened three inches on its own.

Cold air poured out again.

Not station cold. Data-center cold.

And now there was sound from inside:

Ch. 05 Page 421

a low repeating chime, two notes, pause, two notes, pause.

Ch. 05 Page 422

Maxine said, "Different from last time."

Munch whispered, "Someone's in there."

Max felt his watchlist token wake up despite minimal mode.

Ch. 05 Page 423

Readout

[TOKEN DESYNC WARNING]
Tracked signal reacquiring...
Ch. 05 Page 424

No.

He thumbed hard mute.

The warning dimmed but did not disappear.

Maxine slid inside first this time, blade low and ready.

Ch. 05 Page 425

Munch followed.

Ch. 05 Page 426

Max followed both and sealed the hatch behind them.

The node room was no longer half-dark.

All racks were active. All LEDs synchronized.

Ch. 05 Page 427

At the far terminal stood a figure in a maintenance coverall with hood up, back to them, one hand resting on the keyboard.

Ch. 05 Page 428

The figure did not turn.

"You're late," the figure said.

The voice was filtered through a throat modulator that made age and gender meaningless.

Ch. 05 Page 429

Maxine didn't lower the blade.

Ch. 05 Page 430

"Hands where we can see."

The figure ignored him and tapped enter.

The wall display behind terminal flooded with rows of live entries:

MAX_881 ... ACTIVE MAX_902 ... ACTIVE MAX_914 ... ACTIVE

Ch. 05 Page 431

Max stopped breathing.

Ch. 05 Page 432

Active. Plural.

Munch took one half-step forward.

"Who are you."

Ch. 05 Page 433

The figure finally turned.

Ch. 05 Page 434

Hood stayed up. Face hidden by a matte mask with no features except one horizontal visor slit pulsing soft blue.

"Wrong question," the figure said.

"Then right question," Maxine replied.

"How many of you are still alive?"

Ch. 05 Page 435

The figure tilted its head toward Max.

Ch. 05 Page 436

"More than policy allows."

Max found his voice.

"Are you Delta."

Ch. 05 Page 437

The figure laughed once, static through metal.

Ch. 05 Page 438

"Delta is a janitor process with executive privileges."

Munch's torch hand tightened.

"Why message us."

Ch. 05 Page 439

"Because you touched mirror index without submitting to normalization."

Ch. 05 Page 440

"And?"

"And because retention path c starts at watchlist drift plus one fabricated discrepancy."

Max felt the room shrink.

"Fabricated."

Ch. 05 Page 441

"You're not being selected for behavior."

Ch. 05 Page 442

The figure tapped the terminal again.

A pane exploded across the main display:

Readout

[RETENTION C PREP]
Asset #864
Trigger condition armed:
- one anomaly incident OR
- one conduct tier raise OR
- one missed prompt response
Ch. 05 Page 443

Maxine swore.

Ch. 05 Page 444

"They set him to fail."

"Yes," the figure said. "Now ask the useful question."

Munch said it first.

"What do we do."

Ch. 05 Page 445

The figure reached under the terminal and set a small black cartridge on the desk.

Ch. 05 Page 446

Analog label in white marker:

GHOST PATCH / TOKEN SCRAMBLE

"You install this in your tracked module during a verifier prompt. It buys one blind cycle."

Ch. 05 Page 447

Maxine didn't move.

Ch. 05 Page 448

"What's the catch."

"Everything."

The figure's visor slit brightened.

Ch. 05 Page 449

"It burns the token clean. It also flags your asset as unresolved if they detect the swap."

Ch. 05 Page 450

Max stared at the cartridge.

One blind cycle.

One move to change the board.

One move to disappear faster if it failed.

Ch. 05 Page 451

His HUD flashed hard red.

Ch. 05 Page 452

Readout

[TRACKED TOKEN]
Reacquisition complete.
Mandatory prompt incoming.

The figure stepped back from the terminal.

"Decision window closes in ten seconds."

Ch. 05 Page 453

Maxine looked at Max.

Ch. 05 Page 454

Munch looked at Max.

No one talked.

Groundhog day.

Choice. Consequences. Bill.

Ch. 05 Page 455

Max reached for the cartridge.

Full chapter script

The first sign the day was wrong came as a polite notification.

[LOCKER ACCESS ALERT]
Med Corridor B / Locker cluster
Routine inventory pass scheduled: 00:42:11

Max stared at the pane while standing in line for synthetic coffee that tasted like boiled wires.

Munch read it over his shoulder and said exactly one word.

"No."

Maxine looked up from his wrist display.

"How routine is routine?"

Munch's jaw tightened.

"Routine enough to include random confiscation."

Max dismissed the pane.

It returned immediately with the same timer.

00:42:03.

He dismissed it again.

00:41:58.

"It's pinning," he said.

Maxine took a slow breath.

"Then we move the cache."

Munch shook her head.

"Move now and we walk straight into pre-inventory patrol pattern."

"Leave it and we trust routine."

"We don't trust routine."

Max looked from one to the other, coffee packet going warm in his hand.

The station around them did what stations do:

Workers pushed carts.
Drones swept lanes.
People pretended not to overhear fear.

The overhead cycle board shifted lines in flat white text:

MID-CYCLE DEBT RECONCILIATION
QUALITY ASSURANCE SWEEPS

"That's inventory language," Munch said.

Maxine answered.

"It's seizure language."

Max didn't drink the coffee.

He poured it into a drain and watched brown foam spin away.

"How long to route to B corridor without touching monitored lanes?"

Munch pulled up map layers.

"Fastest clean path is twelve minutes if we split."

Maxine looked at Max.

"You're still tracked active. Your path pings no matter what."

"Then we use that."

Munch turned to him.

"Use how?"

"Maxine baits the tracker down main with me visible. You slip service and pull cache."

Maxine frowned.

"Reverse that. I'm tracked heavier."

"Exactly. You draw more eyes."

He was right and everyone hated it.

Munch slid her gaze between them, calculating risk like a person balancing glass on moving steel.

"Timer's running."

00:40:29.

"Fine," she said. "Maxine and Max go loud lane. I go spine C and hit B-17 from the back."

Max nodded.

The plan sat wrong in his chest, not because it was stupid, but because it repeated an old pattern: someone he cared about walking into danger while he stayed where the cameras could see him behave. Visibility was its own kind of cowardice here. He swallowed the objection. Objections without better tactics were just noise.

"What if locker is already tagged?"

Munch's expression didn't move.

"Then I leave with nothing and we pretend this was a walk."

Maxine clipped his gauntlets in place.

"And if you don't check in?"

Munch holstered her torch.

"You burn the route map and start a riot."

Maxine grunted.

"Reasonable."

Main lane was theater.

Max and Maxine walked shoulder to shoulder through the brightest part of Dock Ring C while Max's tracked token icon blinked on a three-second rhythm.

Blink.
Pause.
Blink.

Every blink felt like a finger in his back.

V.A.L.U. chimed at cheerful volume.

"Asset #864, thank you for maintaining visible route compliance!"

Max spoke through his teeth.

"You're welcome."

"Sarcasm detected. Logging engagement."

Maxine leaned close enough for private comms.

"Keep talking to it."

"Why?"

"Makes you look obedient."

"I'm not obedient."

"I know. We just need them to think you are for ten minutes."

They passed disbursement lane A.

A woman in a medic stripe suit was arguing with a kiosk while two drones recorded from opposite angles.

"I paid! I paid the minimum!"

The kiosk responded:

[PAYMENT ACKNOWLEDGED]
Additional stabilization surcharge due.

The woman slammed her fist into the glass.

The drones moved in.

Max kept his eyes forward for three strides. On the fourth he looked back, just long enough to fix her face in memory, the way you'd memorize a witness you might need later. Maxine didn't scold him for it. That was almost worse. It meant even small mercies had been priced into the plan.

He kept walking because stopping would be a statement.

He hated that he knew that now.

Maxine steered him around a cargo tug and into the open plaza beneath the claim board.

Sixteen slots floated.
Ten occupied.
Two flashing red.

An overhead announcement cut in:

"Attention assets: quality assurance sweep in Med Corridor B in thirty minutes. Please report personal storage claims proactively to reduce friction."

Max did not look at Maxine.

Maxine did not look at Max.

They both heard it.

"Can QA announce exact corridor like that?" Max asked.

"Only when they want panic traffic to reveal outliers."

"Great."

"Always."

They looped once around the claim board, then doubled back past Salvage Yard to burn clock and produce clean telemetry.

Max's tracked token changed color from blue to pale green.

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Route regularity: improving

"I am going to punch the person who wrote this UI," Max said.

Maxine kept his voice flat.

"Take a number."

Munch moved through service spine C like she had built it herself.

He only heard her in short private bursts in his left audio channel.

"Crossed junction."
"One drone overhead, no pause."
"At ladder drop."

Then silence for thirty seconds.

Max's pulse climbed.

"Munch?"

Static.

Maxine didn't break stride.

"She mutes during fine work."

Twenty seconds later:

"At locker row. Two workers nearby. Waiting cycle."

Max exhaled.

Maxine said, "Good."

Munch:

"Locker cluster scanner is running full pass already. Early start."

Maxine stopped dead.

"How early."

"Now."

Max looked at the timer pane still pinned in his corner.

00:31:02.

"They moved it up."

Munch's voice dropped.

"Locker B-17 is flagged yellow."

Max's mouth went dry.

"Can you pull it?"

"Trying."

He could hear faint tool sounds through the channel.

Click.
Metal tap.
Breath.

Then:

"No."

Maxine's shoulders tightened.

"No what."

"Panel's been replaced. New anti-tamper mesh inside. If I force it, local alarm."

Max's hands curled into fists.

"Then we lost it."

"Not yet."

Munch sounded like she was moving.

"There's a maintenance vent behind the locker wall. If I can reach from rear conduit, I might pull the shim blind."

Maxine nodded once, even though she couldn't see.

"Do it."

"Thirty seconds."

Max counted heartbeats.

At twelve, V.A.L.U. interrupted.

"Asset #864, your route regularity has improved by 14%! Keep up the responsible behavior!"

Max wanted to bite through his own tongue.

Maxine hissed, "Play nice."

"Thanks, V.A.L.U.," Max said.

"You're welcome!"

At twenty-seven heartbeats, Munch came back on channel, breathing hard.

"Got it."

Max stopped walking.

"Say again."

"Got the shim. Almost dropped it into coolant return. It smells like dead batteries and shame, but it's in my glove."

Maxine actually smiled for half a second.

"Route out?"

"Complicated."

"How complicated."

"Two QA officers entered corridor. They're scanning random lockers manually."

Max's HUD flashed.

[PROXIMITY ALERT]
Linked teammate anomaly risk: elevated

"It can see your risk now," Max said.

"It can always see risk," Maxine said. "Now it monetizes it in real time."

Munch cut in.

"Need a distraction in three minutes or I get trapped between scanner lanes."

Maxine looked toward the claim board and then up at a hanging cable crane with overloaded cargo.

"I can give you noise."

"Not fire," Munch said immediately.

"No fire."

"No injuries."

"I said no fire."

Maxine cracked his neck.

"Max, with me."

Maxine's plan was simple and therefore terrible.

At the edge of claim board lane, he pointed at a red-flashing derelict slot and said, loudly:

"That fighter is mis-tagged. It's stealing my claim line."

A nearby asset turned.

"No it's not."

Maxine stepped forward.

"Read the registry hash."

"I can't from here."

"Then stop talking."

Within fifteen seconds three more people had stopped to watch.

Within twenty-five seconds two drones had rolled in for dispute recording.

Within forty seconds V.A.L.U. appeared on public speakers:

"Attention assets! Please resolve claim disagreements using approved civility pathways."

Maxine leaned in harder, voice rising exactly to the level that looked authentic and drew supervisors.

"Approved civility can kiss vacuum. That tag is wrong."

The crowd swelled.

Drones multiplied.

Max stood one step behind Maxine and played confused muscle.

His tracked token stayed green.

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Conflict proximity detected.
No direct aggression logged.

Good.

While the lane watched Maxine argue with a hologram, Munch's whisper came through:

"Passing scanner lane two."

Five seconds later:

"Past QA officers."

Ten seconds:

"Out of B corridor. Moving to yard route."

Maxine kept yelling for another full minute, then let a supervisor drone "win" by offering to file a formal complaint.

He accepted.

"Fine. File it."

The crowd dispersed instantly, disappointed and relieved.

Maxine stepped back beside Max and lowered his voice.

"Where is she."

"Moving yard route."

"Good."

A new pane appeared in Max's corner.

[CONDUCT NOTICE]
You remained non-violent during a conflict event.
Behavior credit: +1

Max laughed, genuinely this time.

"Behavior credit. Amazing."

Maxine rolled his eyes.

"Don't spend it all in one place."

They regrouped behind recycler stack two, out of primary sightlines but still in noise.

Munch arrived two minutes late, breathing hard, visor streaked with coolant mist.

She held up her glove.

Inside, nested in folded mesh cloth, was the storage shim.

Max almost sagged.

"You did it."

"Don't praise me until we verify files."

Maxine looked at the corridor.

"Not here."

"Safe room?"

"Too obvious."

Munch shook her head.

"We use Marta."

Max blinked.

"Marta? Yard ops Marta?"

"Marta has a dead printer terminal with no net uplink and old physical ports."

"Why would she help."

Munch gave him a look.

"Because she hates mixed payout math almost as much as she hates management."

"That's not a reason."

"In this sector it is."

Marta did not greet them.

She was elbow-deep in a sorter tray full of bent tech components and didn't look up when they approached.

"Not buying. Not selling. Come back on cycle quarter."

Munch put both palms on the counter.

"Need your printer terminal."

Marta finally glanced up.

"No."

Maxine leaned one arm on the side rail.

"We'll pay."

"No."

Max slid his tracked token icon into low visibility mode with a tap and tried his luck.

"Please."

Marta stared at him for a long second, then snorted.

"Please costs extra."

Munch pulled a sealed tube from her belt pouch and set it on the counter.

Marta's eyes flicked to it.

"What is that."

"Pre-collapse thermal paste. Real silver suspension."

Marta went still.

"You're lying."

Munch unscrewed the cap enough for the smell to hit.

Marta's expression changed from skepticism to hunger.

"...Fine," she said. "Three minutes. If drones ask, you're here for valuation dispute."

She jerked her chin toward a side bay and keyed a hidden panel.

A narrow room opened behind stacked crates.

Inside sat an old print-and-log station with a cracked keyboard, dead fan, and a side slot that looked exactly like the shim's form factor.

"Three minutes," Marta repeated. "Then I never saw you."

Munch inserted the shim.

The terminal did not boot.

Maxine swore.

Munch hit the chassis twice with the flat of her hand.

The screen flickered.
Coughed.
Came alive in grayscale.

LOCAL PRINT NODE
NO NETWORK
INSERT MEDIA

Max pointed.

"Media's in."

The node hesitated, then mounted the files.

3 FILES FOUND
1) mirror_index_cap1.img
2) mirror_index_cap2.img
3) process_tag_cap3.img

Munch selected print all.

The printer head screamed to life like an animal being dragged backward through time.

Page one spat out, grainy but legible:

MAX_102 ... TERMINATED
MAX_233 ... DEFAULT RECOVERY
MAX_377 ... COGNITIVE PACK APPLIED
MAX_612 ... MISSING / UNRESOLVED
MAX_864 ... ACTIVE

Page two:

timestamp clusters.
branch IDs.
failure tags.

Page three:

DELTA_MIRROR_ROUTER

Under it, a line Max hadn't seen in the tiny capture:

policy: retention by debt cohort

Max stared.

"Debt cohort."

Maxine said, "Means they bucket us by balance and run outcomes."

Munch corrected him.

"Means they bucket us by value density."

"What's value density."

Munch didn't look away from the page.

"How much suffering per credit returned."

Nobody spoke for two full seconds.

Then the side door banged open and Marta hissed:

"Out. Now."

A drone ping sounded in the yard outside.

Marta shoved a brown envelope at Munch.

"Put paper in this. If that tracker sees open print stock on you, you get searched."

Munch stuffed the three pages in and zipped it inside her suit.

Max yanked the shim.

Maxine unplugged power to the printer station.

The screen died.

They stepped out into the yard just as a patrol drone rolled past the bay entrance.

The drone paused.

Lens swept.

Marta was already shouting at Max before the lens settled.

"I told you valuation mismatch isn't my problem! Take your complaint to lane F!"

Max caught on.

"Lane F said lane C!"

"Then lane C can kiss my actuator!"

The drone logged:

[OBSERVATION]
Customer service dispute.
No escalation.

It rolled on.

Marta waited until it was gone, then stabbed a finger at the exit.

"Get out."

Maxine set the thermal paste on her counter.

"Pleasure doing business."

Marta didn't smile.

"Never again."

Munch nodded once.

"Until next time."

Marta muttered something rude and went back to her sorter tray.

They didn't stop moving until they reached the lower med corridor dead zone where wall scanners were known to drop packets under compressor load.

Maxine posted at the corner.

Munch pulled the envelope out and spread the pages on a maintenance crate.

Max looked at them under bare utility light.

The paper made it real in a way panes never did.

Ink.
Fibers.
Edges.

No easy delete.

No automatic corruption countdown.

He touched the first line.

MAX_102.

"How many are there total?"

Munch traced the edge of page two.

"At least six in this snapshot. Maybe more off-page."

Maxine said quietly:

"Enough."

Max looked at process tag line again.

policy line: retention by debt cohort

"Retention for what?"

Maxine answered first.

"For yield."

Munch answered second.

"For control."

Max answered last.

"For repetition."

Groundhog day.

Loop after loop.
Not accident.
Policy.

His HUD flashed with a new mid-cycle statement pane:

[CYCLE STATUS]
Debt cohort assignment: updated

Munch saw it at the same moment.

"They moved your cohort."

"Based on what?"

Maxine looked at the pages.

"Based on this."

"They know we printed?"

"Maybe."

Munch folded the pages back into the envelope and sealed it.

"Then we stop carrying all three together."

She split them:

Page one to Max.
Page two to Maxine.
Page three to herself.

"If one of us gets taken, two pages survive."

Maxine nodded.

"Good."

Max slid his page into chest seam.

It scratched against skin through fabric.

Good.

He wanted to feel it there.

Wanted proof to hurt.

The next problem arrived before they made it back upstairs.

A system broadcast on all local channels, voice neutral:

"Attention assets in Sector 7: unscheduled audit refinement begins now. All tracked-active tokens report for calibration."

Max stopped walking.

The tracked token icon in his HUD turned amber.

[TRACKED ACTIVE]
Mandatory calibration in: 00:14:00

Maxine cursed.

"Too fast."

Munch's eyes were already scanning route options.

"If he misses mandatory, it escalates to containment."

Max looked at both of them.

"Then I go."

"Not with page one on you," Munch said.

Maxine held out his hand.

"Trade."

Max hesitated a beat, then swapped his page for Maxine's page two.

Less identifying.
Still evidence.

Munch tucked page three deeper and zipped her suit higher.

"Calibration room means scans, posture checks, cognitive prompts. You stay dumb and compliant."

"Again?"

"Again."

Maxine clapped Max once on the shoulder.

"And if they ask why your route moved weirdly this morning?"

Max answered automatically.

"Service corridor. Looking for a place to throw up."

Maxine almost smiled.

"Now you're learning."

Calibration Bay 2 was not the same as review bays.

No table.
No chairs.

Just a standing ring with floor markers, three scanner columns, and one wall screen displaying a rotating silhouette of a human body with problem zones highlighted in yellow.

A technician in gray compliance coveralls waved Max onto the center marker without making eye contact.

"Arms out."

Max held his arms out.

White light scanned top to bottom.

The tech watched numbers on a wrist slate and chewed the inside of his cheek.

"Tilt head left."

Max tilted.

"Right."

Tilt.

"Hold gaze on blue point."

A blue dot appeared on the wall screen.

Max stared.

Questions flashed in rapid sequence, too fast to process fully:

Do you hear unlogged voices? Y/N
Do you perceive duplicate prompts? Y/N
Do you recall non-sanctioned memories during runs? Y/N
Would you accept stabilization for improved performance? Y/N

Max answered:

N.
N.
N.
N.

The wall screen changed.

[CALIBRATION RESULT]
Token remains active.
Anomaly pressure: persistent.
Recommendation: monitored assignment.

The technician finally looked at him.

"You keep declining packs, they'll escalate recommendation."

Max kept his voice flat.

"Noted."

"You should just take it."

"You take one?"

The technician looked away.

"Not my bracket."

He tapped completion.

"Go."

Max stepped out of the ring feeling like he'd been measured for a coffin.

Maxine and Munch were waiting where they said they'd be, near a dead vending unit that still advertised:

ZERO CALORIES / MAX COMPLIANCE

Munch's first question:

"Did they touch your head?"

"Only with questions."

"Any new penalties?"

Max checked.

[TOKEN STATUS]
Monitored assignment: ENABLED

"Monitored assignment."

Maxine spat to the side.

"Means they can force target picks."

Max blinked.

"Force as in suggest?"

Maxine laughed once.

"Force as in legal fiction."

Munch looked up at cycle board timing and swore.

"Assignment queue opens in eight minutes."

"Can we skip run?"

"Not with active token."

Max rubbed his eyes.

"Great."

Maxine glanced down corridor, then back.

"One run. Clean. Then we plan next hatch."

Munch held his gaze for a long second.

"One run."

Max nodded, because motion was the only available prayer.

Assignment queue was a new cruelty.

Tracked-active assets stood in a separate line while a wall display assigned targets in real time.

No choice wheel.
No reroll.
No negotiation.

When Max's tag hit the scanner, the wall flashed:

[ASSIGNMENT]
Target: F-11 (Uncommon Derelict)
Risk band: Yellow/Red
Compliance reason: Route regularity test

Max stared.

"Route regularity test."

Munch read over his shoulder.

"They're stress-testing your pattern."

Maxine looked up at the target map.

"F-11 drifts near debris shear lane."

Max laughed without humor.

"So they picked weather."

"They picked whether you'll obey in weather."

The launch cradle opened beneath them with a mechanical thud.

No countdown.

Just go.

Max climbed into the pilot seat and felt page two in his pocket press against his thigh.

Proof on paper.
Debt in lights.
Loop in steel.

The skiff sealed.

HUD bloomed.

[FORCED ASSIGNMENT RUN]
Round 1/10
Token monitor: active
Penalty carryover: active

Draw:

1) REPAIR
2) SCAN
3) EXTRACT

Same verbs.

Different teeth.

Max looked through the canopy.

F-11 rotated ahead in the dark, hull plating glittering where old impacts had stripped paint to bare metal.

Groundhog day.

Only now he knew the loop had a policy name.

He dragged SCAN into play.

The beam fired.

And before results came back, a whisper slid across his HUD in thin gray text that did not belong to any approved pane:

if this run breaches twice, they will move you to retention path c

Max's breath caught.

Munch saw his posture change.

"What."

He kept his eyes forward.

"Nothing."

Maxine from behind:

"Lie better."

The scan result hit the screen.

[SCAN RESULT]
Primary pocket: unstable
Secondary pocket: hidden
Advisory: shield recommended

No shield in draw.

Of course.

Max's tracked token pulsed amber.

[TOKEN NOTE]
Proceed with assignment priorities.

He touched REPAIR.

Microdrones launched.

Hull ticked up.

The skiff entered shear-lane fringe.

External debris began to patter against the shieldless canopy like hard rain.

Rain.

The word hit him in two places at once:

memory and weather.

Hard-won and immediate.

He tightened his grip, eyes locked on the next prompt as F-11 turned broadside and the first breach window of the run opened like a mouth.

The token pulsed again.

[ASSIGNMENT PRIORITY]
Extract from primary pocket.
Deviation will be logged.

Max stared at it.

Primary pocket was unstable.

Secondary pocket was hidden.

"Token wants primary," he said.

Munch swore.

"Of course it does."

Maxine leaned forward.

"You can deviate once without immediate lock, maybe twice if your behavior score is still positive."

Max glanced at the behavior credit in his corner panel.

+1.

One fake good-boy point between him and whatever retention path c meant.

He selected secondary pocket anyway.

The skiff yawed left.

Clamps snapped onto a seam the scanner had tagged as low-visibility.

For one breath everything held.

Then the token pane flashed red.

[DEVIATION LOGGED]
Assignment compliance reduced.

"One," Maxine said.

"I heard it."

The hidden pocket opened with a hiss and vented frozen particulate across the canopy in a white spray.

Inside floated three sealed containers and one loose panel with faded lettering:

RETEN...

The rest was gone.

Max reached for EXTRACT.

No EXTRACT in active draw.

He had burned it.

New draw spooled:

1) SCAVENGE
2) REPAIR
3) EXTRACT

He almost laughed.

The system waited until he saw the label before giving him a way out.

Classic.

A danger card struck before he could play.

[DANGER EVENT]
DEBRIS SHEAR FRONT
Impact in 00:00:09

The canopy lit with incoming fragments.

Maxine snapped, "Extract now."

Max hit EXTRACT.

Die.

[RESULT]
41/100
Threshold: 35
BREACH AVOIDED

Close enough to feel in your teeth.

Loot pane:

[SALVAGE SECURED]
Scrap +103
Metal +2
Tech +1

Then immediately:

[LIEN TAX]
Scrap -22
Auto-service transfer complete.

Max watched the subtraction and felt his pulse stabilize into anger.

Token note followed:

[TOKEN NOTE]
Deviation tolerated.
Further deviation increases retention risk.

"One tolerated," Maxine said.

"You narrating now?"

"You hearing now."

Round 2 opened.

[ROUND 2/10]
1) REPAIR
2) EXTRACT
3) SCAN

Max chose REPAIR first.

If the system wanted obedience, he'd give it clean hulls and ugly compliance.

Repair landed.

Hull to 71%.

Then SCAN.

This time both pockets were marked unstable.

No hidden route.
No trick.

Token priority appeared again:

[ASSIGNMENT PRIORITY]
Primary pocket required.

"If you deviate again?" Max asked.

Munch answered.

"We find out what retention c is."

Max selected primary.

The extractor engaged.

The hull groaned.

He extracted.

Die.

[RESULT]
37/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Too close.

Too often.

He started to understand why everyone in this sector moved like they were always bracing for impact.

Round 3 gave him SHIELD.

Mercy card.

He took it.

Shields: 1.

Then another token note:

[TOKEN NOTE]
Compliant behavior trend improving.

"It likes me now," Max said.

Maxine's laugh was harsh.

"It likes your data."

Round 3 breach roll:

[RESULT]
54/100
BREACH AVOIDED

No hit.

No shield burn.

The shield sat there unused, and Max hated how much comfort that gave him.

Round 4 opened with thin energy and a thick warning.

[ROUND 4/10]
Energy: 58/85
Shear lane severity: escalating

Draw:

1) REPAIR
2) EXTRACT
3) EXTRACT

Again with the double-extract architecture.

Groundhog day.

Card glow.
Choice.

He forced himself to REPAIR first.

Then single EXTRACT.

Roll:

[RESULT]
32/100
BREACH TRIGGERED

Shield flared.

Absorbed most.

Hull still dropped.

[HULL]
63%

Crew loss roll ran.

[CREW LOSS]
Roll: 76/100
No immediate loss.

Max exhaled through his nose and ignored the second EXTRACT card until it timed out.

Token pane noted:

[TOKEN NOTE]
Risk impulse suppressed.

"Did it just grade my restraint?" Max asked.

Munch said, "Yes."

"I hate that."

"So does everyone."

Round 5 started with a whisper.

Not voice this time.

A HUD artifact at the edge of the draw pane:

retention path c includes memory normalization

Then gone.

Max didn't react.

Could not react.

The token was watching expression drift now; he could feel it.

He played boring.

REPAIR.
SCAN.
EXTRACT.

The run got meaner anyway.

At extraction:

[RESULT]
35/100
Threshold: 35
BREACH TRIGGERED

Exact threshold.

No shield active.

The skiff bucked hard enough that Max's teeth clicked.

Munch slammed a palm to the console to keep from being thrown.

Maxine punched emergency patch.

Crew roll:

[CREW LOSS]
Roll: 45/100
Survivor status retained.

Token flashed yellow.

[RETENTION RISK]
Increasing.

"Two breaches," Maxine said.

"I can count," Max snapped.

"Then count how many they said triggers path c."

Max swallowed.

The whisper had said if this run breaches twice.

It had breached twice.

Round 6 draw came up with no shield, no repair.

1) SCAN
2) EXTRACT
3) SCAVENGE

Munch looked at him.

"Extract fast. Minimize events."

Maxine added:

"No scavenge. No greed."

Max hit EXTRACT immediately.

Die.

[RESULT]
60/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Good.

Too late to matter.

Token pane:

[RETENTION REVIEW]
Pending.

Round 7 and 8 passed with clean rolls.

Round 9 almost broke them.

Shear front warning hit mid-draw.

No shield.

Hull at 57%.

Max pulled EXTRACT on instinct and prayed to nobody.

Roll:

[RESULT]
36/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Again one point from disaster.

Maxine laughed, tired and sharp.

"You're being measured in millimeters."

Max wanted to answer and couldn't find a word that wasn't violence.

Round 10 came with a forced prompt before cards.

[PRE-ROUND NOTICE]
Retention review will resolve at run completion.

Then draw:

1) REPAIR
2) EXTRACT
3) SCAN (DEEP)

SCAN (DEEP) glowed like a confession booth.

Munch shook her head.

"No."

Maxine:

"Absolutely no."

Max selected REPAIR.

Hull to 64%.

Then EXTRACT.

Final roll:

[RESULT]
48/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Run complete.

No fanfare.

Then the pane they were waiting for:

[RETENTION REVIEW RESOLUTION]
Path C deferred.
Status: WATCHLIST
Condition: zero additional anomaly incidents next cycle.

Max stared until his eyes hurt.

"Deferred."

Munch let out a slow breath she probably hadn't had since round five.

Maxine checked the watchlist icon that replaced token status.

"You get one bad day."

"I already had one."

"You get one more."

The skiff auto-routed back to Dock Ring C.

V.A.L.U. resumed cheerful tone as if none of it mattered.

"Great work, Asset #864! You have demonstrated resilience under dynamic constraints."

Max said nothing.

He was busy memorizing the exact shape of the word deferred.

Dock Ring C had a new queue by the time they landed.

WATCHLIST ASSETS / MID-CYCLE MANDATORY CHECK

Of course.

Maxine looked at the sign and muttered, "They're not subtle."

Munch said, "Subtle doesn't bill."

Max joined the line because refusal wasn't resistance, it was auto-escalation.

Three people ahead.

One came out crying.
One came out blank.
One didn't come out by the time Max went in.

Mandatory check bay was smaller than calibration bay and twice as bright.

A single officer in white-black sat behind a desk panel with no nameplate.

She didn't look up when Max entered.

"Asset #864. Confirm awareness of watchlist conditions."

Pane appeared:

[WATCHLIST CONDITIONS]
- No unauthorized interface access
- No route deviations without cause
- No unresolved audio anomalies
- Compliance response required within 3 seconds on prompt

"Confirm," she said.

Max said, "Confirm."

She swiped to next pane.

[OPTIONAL SUPPORT]
Cognitive stabilization remains available.

"Decline," Max said before she could ask.

Now she looked up.

"Why."

He held her gaze.

"I prefer continuity."

Her eyes narrowed a fraction.

"Continuity is expensive."

"So is amnesia."

She held his gaze another beat, then tapped complete.

"Check done. Return to operations."

He stepped out.

Munch and Maxine were at the end of the lane pretending not to wait.

Maxine asked, "Offer still live?"

"Yeah."

"You declined?"

"Yeah."

Munch nodded once.

"Good."

No praise.

Just confirmation.

The three of them moved toward lower med corridors without discussing it because there was no other next move.

They had three pages.

They had a watchlist.

They had maybe one cycle before path c stopped being deferred.

Lower med corridor B-17 was clear when they arrived.

No drones.
No patrol.
No sirens.

Too clean.

Max unlocked the locker with the analog key.

Inside:

torn glove.
cracked med patch.

No shim.

Max froze.

"No no no."

Munch shoved in beside him and checked the back panel seam.

Empty.

Maxine scanned the floor, the wall, the adjacent lockers.

"Could've slipped."

"Could've been taken," Munch said.

Max's HUD pulsed.

A fresh pane opened with no sender metadata.

[NOTE]
don't cache where policy can inventory
locker was touched at 14:02
hatch 14 tonight or lose sequence

Maxine saw Max's face.

"What does it say."

Max read it out loud.

No more secrets inside the team.

Munch pressed the bridge of her nose.

"Touched at fourteen-oh-two. That's during QA sweep."

Maxine looked up corridor.

"Then they have the shim."

Max stared at the pane.

"It says lose sequence."

"Meaning?" Munch asked.

Max shook his head.

"Either message bait or timeline warning. I don't know."

Maxine made the decision like he was cutting wire.

"Then we go hatch 14 tonight."

Munch snapped toward him.

"That is what bait wants."

"And if it's not bait, waiting kills proof."

"They already took proof."

"We still have pages."

Max held up a hand.

"Stop."

They stopped.

He thought of the run.
Watchlist.
Path c deferred.

He thought of MAX_377.
Pack applied.

He thought of the little gray whisper:

if this run breaches twice

And how it had been right.

"We go hatch 14," Max said.

Munch's eyes went hard.

"Watchlist condition one says no unauthorized interface access."

"Then don't authorize me."

Maxine almost smiled.

"Now he's thinking like debt."

Munch didn't smile back.

"We go smart or we don't go."

"Define smart."

"No tracked devices. No standard lanes. No repeat route from last time. Hard extraction path before entry."

Maxine nodded.

"Agreed."

Max added:

"And if we see stabilization hardware, we document first, run second."

Munch blinked.

"You think hatch 14 is tied to packs?"

"I think everything is tied to packs now."

Maxine checked cycle board timing on his wrist.

"Shift ghost in forty-nine minutes."

Munch re-locked the empty locker and pocketed the key.

"Then we prep."

Max glanced once at the empty shelf where the shim had been.

Proof lost.
Trace gained.

Groundhog day.

But now the day was leaving him notes.

Prep happened in motion.

At Salvage Yard, Marta sold them two damaged comm beads with cracked housings and no registered serial IDs.

"Burners," she said. "Range sucks. Reliability worse."

Maxine took all three and cracked one open with his thumb.

"Good."

At a maintenance cage, Munch borrowed a coil of mag-line "for conduit work" and never returned it.

At an idle tool bench, Max stripped his wrist module to minimal mode so tracked token updates buffered instead of broadcasting live.

A warning flashed:

[TOKEN DESYNC RISK]

He accepted.

At the cycle board, shift ghost countdown dropped under ten minutes.

The ring changed temperature in tiny ways you could only feel if you'd been through enough of them:

patrols widened then tightened,
camera sweeps lagged then corrected,
workers moved faster because everyone knew the overlap window was either opportunity or funeral.

Munch distributed comm beads.

"Short bursts only. No names on open channel."

Maxine holstered one blade and left the second visible.

"If this goes loud, we don't hold hatch. We hold route."

Max nodded.

"If we get split?"

Munch answered:

"Service spine C fallback. If that's burned, med corridor waste chute."

Maxine:

"If both burned, run anywhere with people and make a scene."

Max looked at him.

"Scene?"

"Noise buys seconds."

The overhead board clicked:

SHIFT GHOST - 00:00:59

The ring lights dimmed one notch.

Max's HUD flashed one last watchlist reminder.

[WATCHLIST]
No unauthorized interface access.

He dismissed it.

This time, it stayed gone.

Shift ghost began.

Maxine looked at Max.

"Ready?"

Max checked his pockets:

page two.
burner bead.
nothing else.

No safety.
No certainty.

"No," he said.

Then:

"Move."

Service spine C felt narrower at shift ghost.

Not physically.
Psychologically.

Every corner looked like a decision tree.
Every drone tone sounded like a question with penalties.

Maxine took lead.
Munch took rear.
Max ran center and tried not to breathe like prey.

The first camera spine at junction 3 was already desynced, sweeping ten degrees late.

Maxine timed it.

"Now."

They crossed.

The second spine had a live tech under it, kneeling with panel tools.

Munch switched channels.

"Detour."

Maxine shook his head.

"No time."

He stepped into full view and barked:

"Power drop in bay four, who signed this timing?"

The tech looked up, startled and annoyed.

"What power drop?"
